description_long: This object is a tall, slender obelisk-shaped bollard with a square base and a tapering shaft that culminates in a pointed top. The surface is adorned with engraved Egyptian-style hieroglyphs, giving it a decorative and historical appearance. The material appears to be a light tan stone or concrete, with a matte, slightly rough finish. The base is solid and flat, providing stability, while the shaft is monolithic and unbroken except for the engravings. There are no visible moving parts, connectors, or reflective markings. The overall design suggests it is intended as a decorative barrier or marker in an urban or park setting.
description: A tall, obelisk-shaped bollard with engraved Egyptian-style hieroglyphs and a pointed top, made of light tan stone or concrete.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the obelisk, with hieroglyphs clearly visible on the shaft and base.
description_view_1: This view is nearly identical to the front, with the same engravings and proportions visible.
description_view_2: Side view reveals the same obelisk shape and engravings, with the pointed top and square base still prominent.
description_view_3: Another side view, mirroring the previous, with the same decorative engravings and overall form.
